threatgraph_indicators.description: A process has written a suspicious file to disk. Adversaries may write a malicious file to a commonly trusted directory, use a benign name, or a mismatched file extension. This is done for the sake of evading defenses and observation. Check the activity and surrounding events are expected in your environment.
An executable with two file name extensions was written. Adversaries can masquerade malware with a seemingly safe file extension to evade detection. Review the executable.
User hashes from SAM hive accessed from signed executable with untrusted root authority

A process appears to be using a Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) application in a suspicious manner. This may be an attempt to gather information, gain access, or drop other malicious payloads. Investigate the process tree and the detected process.
A process associated with ransomware overwrote data files.

description: A process accessed system network configuration settings. Adversaries can use this for lateral movement efforts. Review the process tree.
name: GenericSystemNetworkConfigDiscovery

description: A process gathered information about the operating system or hardware. Adversaries can use this to identify system vulnerabilities. Review the process tree.
name: GenericSystemInfoDiscovery

threatgraph_indicators.description: A process accessed system network configuration settings. Adversaries can use this for lateral movement efforts. Review the process tree.

description: A process appears to be suspiciously executing code via Powershell. Investigate the command line and process tree.
